summary.intent: Fix a PR (#479 on moltis-org/moltis) by addressing reviewer comments, resolving conversation threads, and keeping the PR clean.
summary.outcome: Pushed a commit switching to Node 22 LTS via NodeSource with .npm cache volume and updated docs; resolved all review threads including a new one about Docker-in-Docker bind mount path clarification in mcp.md.

repo: Node version is managed via NodeSource in the Dockerfile, targeting Node 22 LTS
.npm cache is added to VOLUME for persistence
Docker-in-Docker setups use host paths for bind mounts, not paths inside the Moltis container — this is documented in docs/src/mcp.md

code.finding: Added clarification that Docker-in-Docker bind mount paths reference the host filesystem, not the Moltis container's paths

workflow: GitHub review threads must be resolved via API (thread IDs), not just by minimizing comments — minimizing is not the same as resolving
Accidentally minimized comments can be unminimized via the GitHub API

friction: Accidentally minimized a review comment instead of resolving the thread, requiring a follow-up API call to unminimize it
Resolving review threads required fetching thread IDs first — not directly accessible without a separate API call
